skill.name: react-ui-patterns
skill.description: Modern React UI patterns for loading states, error handling, and data fetching. Use when building UI components, handling async data, or managing UI states.
skill.summary: Modern React UI patterns for loading states, error handling, and data fetching. Use when building UI...
skill.author: ChrisWiles

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance patterns, but the reviewed evidence is markdown guidance and React examples. No executable scripts, network calls, shell commands, prompt injection attempts, or data exfiltration behavior were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged many markdown code fences and TypeScript or TSX examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The referenced regions are documentation examples for React loading states, errors, buttons, empty states, forms, and anti-patterns, not executable skill scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are fenced markdown examples and prose only. No shell interpreter, Ruby execution, child process call, or runnable helper file is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm indicators at lines 3, 180, and 187. Those lines contain the skill description and user-facing empty state text, with no hashing, encryption, credential handling, or cryptographic API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain text and UI copy. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any other weak cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged system reconnaissance at lines 128 and 219. Both locations are React button properties that disable submission controls during invalid or loading states, not system inspection commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines use UI validation variables such as isValid and loading. No evidence found for OS, network, hostname, process, or environment reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: React interfaces often fail through stale loading states, hidden errors, and repeated submissions. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical patterns for async UI behavior.
actual_capabilities: Guides when to show loading indicators only if no usable data exists.
Defines an error handling hierarchy for inline errors, toasts, banners, and full error screens.
Shows button state rules for disabling triggers during async operations.
Provides empty state requirements for lists, searches, and first-use experiences.
Outlines form submission flow with validation, loading feedback, and error callbacks.
Includes a checklist for reviewing React components before completion.
limitations: It is a guidance-only skill and does not include reusable component files.
Examples are generic and must be adapted to the project's UI library.
It focuses on React UI behavior, not backend API design.
It does not provide automated tests or accessibility audits.

use_cases.title: Stabilize Data Fetching Screens
use_cases.description: Apply clear loading, empty, error, and success states to list and detail views.

use_cases.title: Review Product Workflows
use_cases.description: Check whether user actions provide feedback, prevent duplicate submissions, and recover from failures.

use_cases.title: Create UI Review Standards
use_cases.description: Turn the included checklist into consistent acceptance criteria for component reviews.

prompt_templates.title: Add Basic UI States
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for improving a single component.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React component and add clear loading, error, empty, and success states using the react-ui-patterns guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Async Button Behavior
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for forms and mutations.
prompt_templates.prompt: Update this form so submit buttons disable during async work, show progress, validate before submit, and surface failures to the user.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Cached Data Loading
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for data fetching screens.
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this data fetching screen so cached data stays visible during refetches and loading indicators appear only when no data is available.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a React Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for multi-step product workflows.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this React workflow for stale UI, swallowed errors, missing empty states, duplicate submissions, and weak feedback after user actions.

output_examples.input: A list page shows a spinner every time data refreshes.
output: The existing list should remain visible during background refreshes.
A loading skeleton should appear only before the first usable response.
An empty state should appear only after loading completes with no items.

output_examples.input: A mutation logs errors but does not show users what happened.
output: Surface the failure with a toast or inline message.
Keep the retry action available when recovery is possible.
Avoid treating console output as user-facing feedback.

output_examples.input: A submit button can be clicked repeatedly during form submission.
output: Disable the trigger while submission is in progress.
Show a loading indicator on the button.
Validate the form before calling the mutation.

best_practices: Keep existing data visible while background refreshes run.
Always pair failed async actions with visible user feedback.
Disable action triggers while the related operation is pending.
anti_patterns: Replacing cached content with a spinner during every refetch.
Catching errors only in logs while users see no failure state.
Leaving submit buttons active during pending mutations.

faq.question: Does this skill install React components?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance and review patterns for components in your existing project.

faq.question: Can it work with GraphQL clients?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples use common query and mutation concepts that can map to GraphQL clients.

faq.question: Is it only for web React?
faq.answer: Most guidance applies to React web, and some list and form patterns also fit React Native.

faq.question: Does it replace accessibility testing?
faq.answer: No. It improves UI state behavior but does not perform accessibility validation.

faq.question: When should a spinner be shown?
faq.answer: Show a spinner or skeleton when loading starts and no usable data is available yet.

faq.question: How should errors be displayed?
faq.answer: Choose the smallest visible error surface that helps the user understand and recover.
